meta.purpose: Machine-readable internal linking rules for IoTEdges SEO content across products, solutions, knowledge base, case studies, and blog articles.

field_definitions.source_page_type: Page type where the link rule applies.
field_definitions.required_targets: Minimum target page types to link from the source page.
field_definitions.minimum_links: Minimum number of contextual internal links required.
field_definitions.placement_policy: Where links should appear within the page.

global_rules.description: Every product and solution page must have inbound support from at least one knowledge page, one article, or one case study once those pages exist.
Pillar pages must link down to relevant cluster pages and cluster pages must link back to the pillar.
Important links should appear inside useful explanatory sections, not only in footer-style related link lists.
Use natural anchor text variations instead of repeating the exact same keyword in every internal link.

anchor_text_policy: Use product category names for product links.
Use application names for solution links.
Use protocol or concept names for knowledge links.
Use result-oriented wording for case links.
placement_policy: Link to parent or sibling products in the product overview section.
Link to solutions in the use cases section.
Link to knowledge base pages in protocol/interface sections.
Link to case studies near proof or deployment examples.
Link to buyer/how-to articles near selection guidance.
avoid: Do not link every product page to every other product page.
Do not use identical anchor text repeatedly.
Do not link to unsupported candidate product pages as if they are live.

anchor_text_policy: Use product names where product fit is discussed.
Use protocol names where architecture is discussed.
Use related solution names where a narrower or adjacent application is mentioned.
Use case titles or natural proof-oriented text for case links.
placement_policy: Link products in recommended architecture and product fit sections.
Link knowledge base in protocol and communication sections.
Link case studies in proof/example deployment sections.
Link adjacent solutions in use case expansion sections.
avoid: Do not make solution pages pure sales pages without technical support links.
Do not link to case studies that cannot support the same industry or architecture.
Do not overuse generic anchors such as learn more.

anchor_text_policy: Use exact technical term anchors when defining concepts.
Use practical application anchors when linking to solutions.
Use product names only where product fit is genuinely relevant.
placement_policy: Link related knowledge pages in definitions and comparison sections.
Link products in practical application or gateway selection sections.
Link solutions where the technology is used.
Link how-to or troubleshooting articles after explaining common mistakes.
avoid: Do not turn knowledge pages into product pages.
Do not link to products before explaining the technical concept.
Do not include unverified product capabilities as technical facts.

anchor_text_policy: Use natural anchors that match the sentence context.
Use the exact product name once, then vary anchors naturally.
Use problem/solution anchors for commercial links.
placement_policy: Link knowledge pages near definitions.
Link products near implementation recommendations.
Link solutions near application examples.
Link cases near proof or architecture examples.
avoid: Do not stuff product links into every section.
Do not link to pages that are less relevant than the article's main intent.
Do not use blog articles as thin doorway pages.

anchor_text_policy: Use products used as anchor text in architecture sections.
Use solution page names in challenge and result sections.
Use protocol names in devices/protocol sections.
Use similar-case anchors at the end of the article.
placement_policy: Link products in project snapshot and architecture sections.
Link solutions in challenge and outcome sections.
Link knowledge base in protocol sections.
Link similar cases in the final related cases section.
avoid: Do not publish as a real case without proof.
Do not link to unrelated industries just to increase link count.
Do not invent metrics or customer names.

topic_cluster_rules.notes: This is one of the highest commercial-intent clusters and should be tightly linked.
Use careful LoRa range language and link to LoRaWAN factual card.
Connect factory, building, power meter, Modbus, and MQTT content.
Useful across water, fuel, oil, and remote site content.

first_30_page_link_requirements.rule: Each page in the first 30 plan must include all entries from its required_internal_links field unless the target page is not yet created. If a target page is not created, add it to the publishing dependency list.

before_publishing_product_page: At least one related solution page should exist or be scheduled.
At least one related knowledge base page should exist or be scheduled.
before_publishing_solution_page: At least two product pages should exist or be scheduled.
At least one protocol or technology knowledge page should exist or be scheduled.
before_publishing_case_study: Product pages used in the case should exist or be scheduled.
The related solution page should exist or be scheduled.
Any metrics must be real, anonymized, or clearly conservative.
